title: Toyota VVT-i / Dual VVT-i Cam Timing Diagnosis and Reset
description: Diagnoses Toyota VVT-i and Dual VVT-i (D-4ST) cam phaser faults. Covers P0010/P0011/P0012/P0020/P0021/P0022 codes, OCV (Oil Control Valve) testing, and cam timing adaptation reset after OCV or cam sensor replacement.
common_symptoms: P0010/P0011 intake cam timing over-advanced bank 1
P0012 intake cam timing over-retarded bank 1
physical_solution: Test OCV (Oil Control Valve) resistance and response. Check oil pressure and quality. If OCV clogged with sludge: clean or replace. Cam timing adaptation resets after OCV replacement.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — Mode $01 PID $0E (timing advance) provides indirect VVT monitoring. Actual cam phase angle vs target via Techstream $22 DID to ECM 0x7E0. OCV actuation test: $2F accessible via ELM327 extended session on most 2GR/2AR engines.
preconditions: Oil level correct and oil NOT sludgy (VVT extremely sensitive to oil quality)
No low oil pressure faults active
Record cam timing at idle and 2000 RPM

procedure_steps.description: CHECK OIL FIRST: VVT-i is oil-pressure actuated. If oil is dark/sludgy, clean sludge is the root cause — not the OCV. Drain and refill with Toyota Genuine Motor Oil or 0W-20 synthetic. Clear code and recheck before replacing parts.

Read cam timing: $22 [CAM_TIMING_DID] to ECM 0x7E0. Compare intake cam actual vs target. Targets: idle = 0° (retarded), 2000 RPM part load = 20-40° advanced. Stuck at 0° at all conditions = OCV not moving cam.

OCV actuation test: $2F [OCV_B1_DID] 03 [MAX_ADVANCE_VALUE]. Command cam to full advance. Monitor cam angle change. If cam moves to target = OCV mechanical OK, issue is intermittent oil pressure or adaptation. If cam doesn't move = OCV failed or sludge blockage.

OCV RESISTANCE TEST: Disconnect OCV connector. Measure resistance across OCV solenoid terminals. Spec: 6.9-7.9 ohms at 20°C for most Toyota VVT-i OCV. Open circuit or short = replace OCV. Note: sludge in OCV strainer is more common cause than electrical failure.

OCV STRAINER CLEANING: Remove OCV (single bolt, intake cam cover). Inspect strainer screen on OCV body. If clogged with fine metallic particles or varnish: clean with brake cleaner and fine brush. Reinstall. If screen damaged: replace OCV.
After OCV replacement/cleaning: clear DTCs. Warm engine to operating temp. Verify cam timing moves to commanded position across RPM range. No P0010/P0011/P0012 return after 2 drive cycles.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: VVT-i family: 1GR-FE (4.0L V6), 2GR-FKS/FSE (3.5L V6), 2AR-FKS (2.5L), A25A-FKS (2.5L), M20A-FKS (2.0L). Dual VVT-i controls both intake and exhaust cams on 2GR-FKS/A25A. D-4ST (Direct and Port Injection combined) on A25A — cam timing affects injection switching logic. Oil change interval compliance CRITICAL — sludge at 50k+ miles on non-synthetic oil destroys VVT system. Toyota recommends 0W-20 full synthetic.
